repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: failed strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC span-lattice proposal lacked the current active-set Riesz-Schur finite computation

published_prior_art.cite: SingerCucuringu2010 completion matrix gives finite local low-rank-completion certificates; component estimator KKT systems appear in DoudchenkoImbens2016, BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021, ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021, and AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: closest failed span-lattice kernel, but did not define an adjacent projected Riesz-Schur iff frontier on staggered fixed-rank charts

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 and AtheyImbensQuViviano2025 compare or combine MC/SC/SDiD-style methods but do not state an adjacent ATT-equality iff certificate
ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 and ChoiKwonLiao2024 give inference for individual SDiD/low-rank procedures, not a plug-in law for an adjacent equality diagnostic

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: earlier strictness/minimal-witness language, but under-specified and single-shock rather than the current concrete noncorner staggered/rank witnesses

published_prior_art.cite: BenMichaelFellerRothstein2021 and AbadieLHour2021 show imperfect-fit/penalty issues, not minimal noncorner witnesses for every adjacent SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC gap

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey still omits deterministic low-rank completion certificate precedents, especially SingerCucuringu2010 and Chatterjee2020, which are closer to the rank-r Schur/completion-matrix part of Delta_{a,b,r} than the causal-panel estimator papers alone.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: K_b(P,D)=rowspan(J_b) \ominus rowspan(J_a) is only unambiguous if the lower normal space is nested in the higher one, or if \ominus is explicitly defined as N_b \cap N_a^\perp / a quotient by N_a \cap N_b.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Singer-Cucuringu 2010 and Chatterjee 2020 to Related work and explain why their completion/solvability certificates do not subsume the causal adjacent ATT-equality quotient.
Replace the informal \ominus quotient with an explicit finite-dimensional definition that works without assuming nested normal spaces.
Add one corollary specializing Delta_{SDID,MC,r} to the pure low-rank completion case and showing exactly how it differs from a standard completion-matrix rank test.

recommended_next_step: Patch the quotient definition and add the deterministic matrix-completion comparators; the flagship kernel otherwise survives.
